description: Idea-exploration workflow — research the domain, solicit peer opinions on the raw idea, sketch a tentative plan, adversarially critique it, research again to verify which critiques are real, then exit at a decision checkpoint. Use to determine whether to build an idea. No implementation phases — when you decide yes, run /forgewright:workflow-run greenfield (or feature) to actually build it.

phases.name: peer-opinions
phases.directive: Solicit each available peer's independent opinion on this idea, informed by the research artifact. Dispatch one task per peer that tells them to: (a) read artifacts/research.md for shared context, (b) form their own honest assessment — viability, risks the research missed, prior art they know of, what would change their mind, and whether they would build this themselves, (c) reply with their opinion in their wrightward_ack detail field (one short paragraph per question is fine; the goal is a usable second pair of eyes, not a dissertation). Aggregate every peer's reply into artifacts/peer-opinions.md, one section per peer (heading: their handle; body: their take verbatim). Advance with --result completed and --artifact-path artifacts/peer-opinions.md. **No-peers fallback**: this phase has value only when opinions come from PEERS, not from leader self-monologue. If wrightward_bus_status reports zero peers, do NOT execute the task yourself. Write artifacts/peer-opinions.md with a single line ('No peers available; opinion phase skipped.'), advance with --result completed --artifact-path artifacts/peer-opinions.md --mcp-result '{"tasks":[]}'.

phases.name: verify-critique
phases.instruction: Invoke the "agentwright:research" skill via the Skill tool. This phase consumes the "critique" artifact — read artifacts/critique.md before invoking the skill. The research target is: for each critique point, investigate whether the critique's underlying claim holds up against current literature, prior art, and real-world evidence. Remember the critique agent (party-pooper) is intentionally adversarial — it is built to surface every possible objection, so some entries will be complaints for the sake of complaining, theoretical risks that don't apply in practice, or worst-case framings of routine tradeoffs. Apply critical thinking: a critique is only as strong as the evidence behind it. Score each critique as VALID (the concern is substantiated by independent evidence), PARTIAL (some merit but overstated or context-dependent), or INVALID (the critique's premise is wrong, the concern is theoretical-only, or the evidence contradicts it). Write the result to artifacts/critique-verification.md as a per-critique table with the verdict and citations, then report the path back via workflow-advance --artifact-path <path>. When done, call workflow-advance --result completed.

phases.name: decision
phases.summary: Idea exploration complete. Artifacts: research.md, plan.md, critique.md, critique-verification.md. User reviews and decides. If you decide to build, run /forgewright:workflow-run greenfield (or feature for an existing codebase). If not, /forgewright:workflow-stop this workflow to mark it cancelled.
